POMDPPlanners

POMDPPlanners
  • <no title>
  • View page source

Tests for Push POMDP environment.

This module tests the Push POMDP environment, focusing on: - Basic environment functionality - Obstacle collision penalties - Reward verification between obstacle and non-obstacle states

class POMDPPlanners.tests.test_environments.test_push_pomdp.TestPushPOMDP[source]

Bases: object

Test cases for Push POMDP environment.

setup_method()[source]

Set up test environment before each test method.

test_action_space()[source]

Test that action space is correct.

test_both_robot_and_object_obstacle_collision()[source]

Test that both robot and object collisions apply penalties.

test_config_id()[source]

Test config_id behavior.

test_environment_equality()[source]

Test environment equality comparison.

test_environment_initialization()[source]

Test that Push POMDP environment initializes correctly.

test_initial_state_distribution()[source]

Test initial state distribution.

test_is_equal_observation()[source]

Test observation equality comparison.

test_object_obstacle_collision_penalty()[source]

Test that object collision with obstacles applies penalty.

test_observation_model()[source]

Test observation model creation and functionality.

test_observation_model_empty_observation_error()[source]

Test that observation model properly handles invalid empty observations.

Purpose: Validates that the observation probability method handles invalid inputs gracefully

Given: A valid PushObservation model and an empty observation array When: The probability method is called with an empty observation Then: A descriptive error should be raised explaining the invalid observation format

Test type: unit

test_observation_model_functionality()[source]

Test observation model functionality with noise.

test_observation_model_invalid_observation_shapes()[source]

Test observation model with various invalid observation shapes.

Purpose: Validates that observation model handles all invalid observation shapes properly

Given: A valid PushObservation model and observations with incorrect shapes When: The probability method is called with malformed observations Then: Appropriate errors should be raised for each invalid shape

Test type: unit

test_observation_never_empty_from_sample()[source]

Test that observation model never produces empty observations from sample method.

Purpose: Validates that the sample method always produces correctly shaped observations

Given: A valid PushObservation model When: Multiple observations are sampled Then: All observations should have shape (6,) and valid content

Test type: unit

test_obstacle_collision_detection()[source]

Test that obstacle collision detection works correctly.

test_reward_difference_obstacle_vs_safe()[source]

Test that rewards in obstacle states are lower than in safe states.

test_reward_function()[source]

Test reward function behavior.

test_reward_range()[source]

Test that reward range is correctly calculated.

test_robot_obstacle_collision_penalty()[source]

Test that robot collision with obstacles applies penalty.

test_sample_next_step()[source]

Test sample next step functionality.

test_sample_next_step_observation_never_empty()[source]

Test that sample_next_step never produces empty observations.

Purpose: Validates that the high-level sample_next_step method produces valid observations

Given: A valid PushPOMDP environment and various states When: sample_next_step is called multiple times Then: All returned observations should be properly shaped and non-empty

Test type: integration

test_state_transition()[source]

Test state transition with pushing behavior.

test_state_transition_model()[source]

Test state transition model creation and functionality.

test_state_transition_no_push()[source]

Test state transition without pushing when robot is too far.

test_terminal_condition()[source]

Test terminal condition detection.
